qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a list of the software available on your national systems?
qa_pairs.answer: A current list of the software available on our national systems is provided.

qa_pairs.question: How often is the list of available software updated?
qa_pairs.answer: The list of available software changes frequently as new software is added.

qa_pairs.question: How can I request the installation or updating of a program or library?
qa_pairs.answer: You can request the installation or updating of a particular program or library by contacting technical support.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if I want to use your software environment on my own system?
qa_pairs.answer: If you wish to use our software environment on your own system, please see the 'Accessing CVMFS' page.

qa_pairs.question: Is all software listed guaranteed to be available in any standard environment?
qa_pairs.answer: No, some software listed may not be available in the specific standard environment (StdEnv) you have loaded.

qa_pairs.question: Is StdEnv/2020 still supported on newer systems?
qa_pairs.answer: No, StdEnv/2020 is now deprecated and hidden on the newer systems.

qa_pairs.question: How do users typically access most software on the national systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Except for basic system programs, you access most software by loading a module.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more information about using the Lmod module system?
qa_pairs.answer: For more on how to use the Lmod module system, see the 'Using modules' page.

qa_pairs.question: Are any prerequisite modules loaded automatically?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, some prerequisite modules are loaded by default.

qa_pairs.question: How are most Python modules provided on your systems?
qa_pairs.answer: Most Python modules are not installed as (Lmod) modules; they are instead provided as binary wheels, stored under `/cvmfs/soft.computecanada.ca/custom/python/wheelhouse/`.

qa_pairs.question: Can you give an example of a Python package provided as a binary wheel?
qa_pairs.answer: TensorFlow is one such package provided as a binary wheel.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions on how to install or list Python packages?
qa_pairs.answer: For instructions on how to install or list Python packages, see the 'Python' page.

qa_pairs.question: Are R or Perl packages installed as Lmod modules?
qa_pairs.answer: No, most R or Perl packages are not installed as (Lmod) modules.

qa_pairs.question: Where are R or Perl packages recommended to be installed?
qa_pairs.answer: We recommend installing R or Perl packages in your personal or group file space.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find instructions for installing R or Perl packages?
qa_pairs.answer: See the 'R' and 'Perl' pages for instructions on how to do so.

qa_pairs.question: Is information available regarding symbolic algebra software?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, a page discusses symbolic algebra software like Mathematica and Sage.

qa_pairs.question: Is Docker available on your clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: No, Docker is not available on our clusters.

qa_pairs.question: What is an alternative to Docker available on your clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Apptainer is available on our clusters by loading the module `apptainer`.

qa_pairs.question: Can Docker containers be converted for use with Apptainer?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, Docker containers can be converted to Apptainer.

qa_pairs.question: Why might some software packages not be immediately usable?
qa_pairs.answer: Some software packages are not immediately usable because they require you to have a license, and you may need to be granted access.

qa_pairs.question: How do I get instructions for obtaining access to licensed software?
qa_pairs.answer: Attempting to load the module for licensed software will give you instructions on what to do to obtain access.

qa_pairs.question: Are all software packages accessible on all servers?
qa_pairs.answer: While the vast majority of software packages are accessible on all our servers, a few are only available at one site or another due to licensing restrictions.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find information about site-specific software?
qa_pairs.answer: See the 'Site-specific software' section for information about software only available at certain sites.

qa_pairs.question: How are the listed software packages organized in terms of environments?
qa_pairs.answer: The packages listed are available in one or more standard software environments.

qa_pairs.question: When might it be necessary to load a different standard environment?
qa_pairs.answer: In rare circumstances, it may be necessary to load a different standard environment (StdEnv) to access a particular version of a particular package.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more details about standard software environments?
qa_pairs.answer: For more on this, please read the 'Standard software environments' page.

qa_pairs.question: Are operating system-related packages like Autotools and Git listed in the software table?
qa_pairs.answer: No, many packages related to the operating system, such as Autotools, Make, and Git, are part of the default environment and are not listed.

qa_pairs.question: What information is found in the 'List of globally-installed modules' table?
qa_pairs.answer: The table lists software for which an environment module has been installed on our systems.

qa_pairs.question: How do I configure my environment using a module from the list?
qa_pairs.answer: In simple cases, the module name listed in the `Module` column can be used with the `module load` command to configure your environment.

qa_pairs.question: Do I always only need to load one module for a software package?
qa_pairs.answer: No, in more complicated cases, some prerequisite modules may also need to be loaded.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find prerequisites and brief notes about a software package?
qa_pairs.answer: Click on the corresponding `[Expand]` link in the `Description` column for a list of prerequisites and brief notes about the software.

qa_pairs.question: How can I access more extensive documentation for a package?
qa_pairs.answer: If more extensive documentation about a package is available, there will be a link in the `Documentation` column.

qa_pairs.question: How can I sort the list of globally-installed modules?
qa_pairs.answer: You can sort the list by clicking the double arrows in the column heading to sort in ascending or descending order for a given column.

qa_pairs.question: Which sorting option might be particularly useful for the software list?
qa_pairs.answer: Sorting by software type might be of particular interest.

qa_pairs.question: What categories are used to tag software in the 'Type' column?
qa_pairs.answer: The `Type` column shows software tagged as: ai (artificial intelligence), bio (biology, bioinformatics), chem (chemistry), geo (earth sciences), io (input/output), math (mathematics), mpi (MPI), phys (physics and engineering), tools (languages and libraries), and vis (visualization).

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find software for legacy architectures like AVX and SSE3?
qa_pairs.answer: Software for legacy architectures can be found on their respective pages: AVX and SSE3.

qa_pairs.question: Where is most application software installed?
qa_pairs.answer: Most application software is installed in CVMFS.

qa_pairs.question: What is CVMFS and why is it used?
qa_pairs.answer: CVMFS is a distributed file system which makes central management of many packages easier.

qa_pairs.question: Are all application software packages installed in CVMFS?
qa_pairs.answer: No, certain packages are not installed in CVMFS but are installed only at some sites, or are installed separately at each site.

qa_pairs.question: Why are some packages installed only at specific sites or separately at each site?
qa_pairs.answer: This is usually due to license restrictions on the package in question.
